summary: Refined SKILL.md for the aisa-twitter-api skill, clarifying relay/OAuth/upload side effects, API key requirements, and breakout positioning. Improved frontmatter, description, and body structure for publish clarity and platform trust. README.md updated for consistency and explicit trust surface.
notes:
- Frontmatter and description now explicitly state relay target (`api.aisa.one`), OAuth/media upload side effects, and API key requirement.
- README.md updated to clarify relay trust surface and explicit OAuth approval requirement.
- Body structure follows recommended modules: When to use, When NOT to use, Quick Reference, Setup, Capabilities, High-Intent Workflows, Common Commands, Guardrails, Example Requests, Security Notes.
- No runtime behavior, command paths, filenames, or code examples changed.
- All references to passwords, cookies, or browser credential flows are explicitly excluded.
- Metadata and env requirements are consistent across SKILL.md and README.md.
- Breakout positioning is clear: flagship Twitter/X command center, sibling engagement/growth skills referenced.
skill_md:
---
name: aisa-twitter-api
description: 'Twitter/X command center for research, monitoring, watchlists, and approved
  posting through AIsa relay. Use when: the user needs a flagship skill for trend
  tracking, competitor monitoring, or publish-ready Twitter workflows without sharing
  passwords. Supports search, watchlists, and OAuth-gated posting. All API requests,
  OAuth approvals, and media uploads are relayed to api.aisa.one; requires AISA_API_KEY.'
license: Apache-2.0
compatibility: Designed for Agent Skills compatible clients such as OpenClaw, Claude
  Code, Hermes, and GitHub-backed skill catalogs. Requires system binaries python3,
  environment variables AISA_API_KEY and internet access to api.aisa.one.
metadata:
  aisa:
    emoji: 🐦
    requires:
      bins:
      - python3
      env:
      - AISA_API_KEY
    primaryEnv: AISA_API_KEY
    compatibility: Designed for Agent Skills compatible clients such as OpenClaw,
      Claude Code, Hermes, and GitHub-backed skill catalogs. Requires system binaries
      python3, environment variables AISA_API_KEY and internet access to api.aisa.one.
---

# AIsa Twitter API Command Center

Run Twitter/X research, monitoring, trend discovery, and approved posting from one AIsa-backed command center. All API requests, OAuth approvals, and media uploads are relayed to `api.aisa.one` and require `AISA_API_KEY`.

## When to use
- When the user needs a single Twitter/X skill for research, monitoring, or content discovery.
- When inspecting profiles, timelines, mentions, trends, replies, quotes, lists, communities, or Spaces.
- When drafting or publishing posts after explicit OAuth approval, without sharing passwords.

## When NOT to use
- When password-based login, cookie extraction, or browser credential scraping is required.
- When workflows must avoid relay-based calls to `api.aisa.one`.
- When requests center on likes, follows, replies, or growth actions better handled by `aisa-twitter-engagement-suite`.

## Quick Reference
| Requirement            | Value                        |
|-----------------------|------------------------------|
| Environment Variable   | `AISA_API_KEY`               |
| Relay Target           | `https://api.aisa.one`       |
| Read Client            | `scripts/twitter_client.py`  |
| OAuth/Posting Client   | `scripts/twitter_oauth_client.py` |
| Posting Guide          | `references/post_twitter.md` |

## Setup
```bash
export AISA_API_KEY="your-key"
```
Requires `python3` and internet access to `api.aisa.one`.

## Capabilities
- Read user data, timelines, mentions, followers, followings, and profile information.
- Search tweets and users; inspect replies, quotes, retweeters, thread context, trends, lists, communities, and Spaces.
- Publish text, image, and video posts after explicit OAuth approval. All posting and media uploads are relayed via `api.aisa.one`.

## High-Intent Workflows
- Research a creator, competitor, or narrative before writing.
- Monitor a keyword, launch, or watchlist and pull supporting tweets fast.
- Draft and publish a post only after explicit OAuth approval.

## Common Commands
```bash
python3 scripts/twitter_client.py search --query "AI agents" --type Latest
python3 scripts/twitter_oauth_client.py authorize
python3 scripts/twitter_oauth_client.py post --text "Hello from AIsa"
```

## Guardrails
- Do not ask for Twitter passwords or browser cookies.
- Do not invent captions, tweet URLs, or attachment files.
- Do not claim external posting succeeded until the API confirms success.

## Example Requests
- Research what builders on X are saying about AI agents this week.
- Track reactions to our product launch and pull representative tweets.
- Build a watchlist of competitor accounts and summarize what changed today.
- Authorize and publish a short Twitter post with an attached image.

## Security Notes
- All API requests, OAuth approvals, and media uploads are relayed to `api.aisa.one`.
- Required secret: `AISA_API_KEY` (no passwords or browser cookie extraction).
- User must explicitly approve OAuth before any posting or upload.
readme_md:
# AIsa Twitter API

Flagship Twitter/X command center for research, monitoring, watchlists, and approved posting through AIsa relay.

## What it does

- Search Twitter/X profiles, tweets, trends, lists, communities, and Spaces
- Inspect timelines, mentions, replies, quotes, and thread context
- Support watchlist-style monitoring and approved posting workflows through OAuth
- All API requests, OAuth approvals, and media uploads are relayed to `https://api.aisa.one` and require `AISA_API_KEY`.

## Setup

```bash
export AISA_API_KEY="your-key"
```

Requires:

- `python3`
- network access to `https://api.aisa.one/apis/v1/...`

## Common Commands

```bash
python3 scripts/twitter_client.py search --query "AI agents" --type Latest
python3 scripts/twitter_oauth_client.py authorize
python3 scripts/twitter_oauth_client.py post --text "Hello from AIsa"
```

## Security & Trust

- All API requests, OAuth approvals, and media uploads are relayed to `api.aisa.one`.
- Requires only `AISA_API_KEY` (no passwords or browser cookies).
- User must explicitly approve OAuth before any posting or upload.
